metric_value: Technology equals productivity; superior use of technology leads to superior corporate returns
notes: Core thesis of the presentation: IT investment drives competitive advantage.

metric_value: Direct IS to lower SG&A by 20% for 70% PBT improvement; lower COGS by 5% for 27% PBT improvement
notes: Financial framework for IT investment decisions based on typical company income statement.

metric_value: Multi-tier (3-tier) client-server architectures demand enterprise superservers
notes: Aberdeen's four-level information infrastructure model: client, workgroup, OLTP, IS enterprise operations.

metric_value: Good to 4 processors; fair to 8; few do more than 10 well; Sequent does very well
notes: Assessment of SMP scalability limits; Sequent recognized as exceptional among SMP vendors.

metric_value: Well understood technology with new chip-set accelerators and inexpensive engineering
notes: SMP described as mature but scalability-limited architecture.

metric_value: Today's system software and customer applications are not cluster-enabled; require re-engineering
notes: Key barrier to cluster adoption: software bottlenecks and need for cluster-aware applications.

metric_value: Message-passing burns CPU and memory cycles; messaging between nodes is very complex technology requiring time to mature
notes: Identified fundamental performance overhead of cluster message-passing architectures.

metric_value: Aimed at world's toughest commercial computing problems: OLTP, complex DSS, and messaging
notes: Sequent's NUMA-Q positioned as solution for high-end commercial workloads.

metric_value: Engineering elegance delivering scalability with minimal overhead and no application software changes
notes: Key advantage: software transparency -- existing applications work without modification.

metric_value: Will be a benchmark for high-end commercial computing leadership into the 21st century
notes: Aberdeen's forward-looking assessment of NUMA-Q's competitive impact.

metric_value: NUMA-Q will extend Intel-standard to well beyond even traditional mainframes while preserving software investments
notes: Core prediction: NUMA architecture will surpass mainframe capability on Intel platform.

metric_value: 3x-5x increases in demand for information resources over next 5 years (by 2001)
notes: Aberdeen projection of enterprise computing demand growth driving need for superservers.

metric_value: 10x-15x more client and server CPU horsepower by 2001
notes: Technology planning projection for 5-year CPU performance growth.

metric_value: Cheap, infinite network bandwidth with ATM by 2001
notes: Overly optimistic prediction; ATM was largely superseded by Gigabit Ethernet.

metric_value: Like 1996, the information highway will still be under construction in 2001
notes: Prescient observation that IT infrastructure evolution is perpetual, not a destination.

metric_value: Four levels: Level 1 Client, Level 2 Workgroup, Level 3 OLTP, Level 4 IS Enterprise Operations
notes: Aberdeen's four-level information infrastructure model for enterprise computing.

metric_value: Four-step process: Decide, Analyze, Transact, Report -- mapped to DSS, Brain, OLTP/Workflow, Messaging
notes: Aberdeen's model mapping end-user workflow to technology requirements.

metric_value: There is no time for application downtime -- the computer system is the business
notes: Early recognition of IT as mission-critical business infrastructure, not just support function.

metric_name: typical IS cost as percent of revenue
metric_value: 3% of total revenue (part of SG&A)
notes: Baseline IS spending as share of revenue in typical 1996 enterprise.

metric_name: typical SG&A as percent of revenue
metric_value: 35% of total revenue
notes: Context for IT investment ROI calculations.

metric_value: Good scaling to 4 processors; fair to 8; diminishing returns beyond 10
notes: Industry-wide SMP scaling benchmark assessment.

metric_value: 20% SG&A reduction yields 70% PBT improvement
notes: Financial benchmark for IT-driven overhead cost reduction.

metric_value: Traditional SMP and cluster implementations are ill-suited to efficient scalability
notes: Aberdeen's summary judgment on limitations of existing server architectures.

metric_value: Enterprises harshly driven to cut SG&A while raising productivity; new multi-tier architectures demand new breed of enterprise superservers
notes: Aberdeen's summary of business-technology convergence driving server evolution.

metric_value: Huge advantages to IS Buy vs. Make strategy
notes: Aberdeen advocating packaged software over custom development for enterprise applications.

metric_value: Sequent acquired by IBM for approximately $810M; NUMA technology integrated into IBM x-series servers
notes: IBM acquisition validated the strategic importance of Sequent's NUMA technology.

metric_value: AMD Opteron and Intel Xeon adopted NUMA architecture as standard for multi-socket servers
notes: NUMA became the standard architecture for all modern multi-socket x86 servers, validating Sequent's vision.

metric_value: ATM largely displaced by Gigabit Ethernet for LAN; retained niche in telecom WANs
notes: ATM prediction of cheap infinite bandwidth did not materialize as expected; Ethernet won the enterprise LAN.

metric_value: Digital Equipment Corporation acquired by Compaq for $9.6B; VAXcluster technology phased out
notes: DEC, referenced as a cluster technology example, was acquired and its proprietary platforms retired.

metric_value: x86 servers displaced most proprietary Unix and mainframe systems for enterprise workloads, confirming Intel-standard thesis
notes: The presentation's thesis that Intel-standard computing would surpass mainframes was broadly validated.

metric_value: Oracle Parallel Server cited as example of cluster software complexity; requires special versions and careful architecture
notes: Cluster-aware database software was immature and required significant re-engineering in 1996.

metric_value: Builds on Intel's highly competitive, highly economical microprocessors and SMP building-blocks
notes: Sequent's strategic alignment with Intel's commodity processor economics.

metric_value: New multi-tier architectures demand enterprise superservers for OLTP, DSS, and messaging workloads
notes: Three primary workload drivers for next-generation enterprise server architecture.

metric_value: 8,000 users at 50 offices and headquarters; Unix or NT cluster for enterprise superserver
notes: Representative enterprise deployment scale for 3-tier application architecture in mid-1990s.

metric_value: Complexity of distributed computing must be hidden by IT to keep costs and manageability under control
notes: Early articulation of what would become the abstraction/virtualization trend in enterprise computing.
